howItWorks.intro: This water purification calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Water = People × Days × Gallons per Person per Day
Boiling: 1 minute rolling boil (3 min above 6,500 ft elevation)
Bleach: 2 drops per liter of 8.25% sodium hypochlorite
Tablets: 1 per liter, 30 minute contact time
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much water do I need per day in a survival situation?
faq.answer: Minimum 1 gallon (3.8L) per person per day for drinking and basic hygiene. In hot weather or with physical activity, increase to 1.5-2 gallons. Children, nursing mothers, and ill people need more.

faq.question: What is the safest water purification method?
faq.answer: Boiling is the most reliable method — it kills all pathogens. Filters remove bacteria and parasites but may miss viruses. Chemical treatment (tablets/bleach) is lightweight but takes time. UV works on clear water only.

faq.question: Can I drink rainwater without purification?
faq.answer: Freshly collected rainwater in clean containers is generally safe, but rooftop collection can contain contaminants. Always purify water from streams, lakes, or questionable sources.

faq.question: Is the Water Purification Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Water Purification Calculator?
sections.body: The Water Purification Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your number of people, number of days, gallons per person per day, you get instant results including total water needed, purification requirements, supply needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total water needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your number of people and need to find the right total water needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Number of People
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your number of people — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of days, gallons per person per day, purification method. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total water needed, purification requirements, supply needed. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your number of people before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total water needed and purification requirements — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your purification method, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
